name: RPlog_I_Was_Only_Passing_Through
title: Interrogation of a Caspian Marine
summary: In an Imperial detention block, Rear Admiral Cimber, a coldly polished officer known for his harsh treatment of prisoners, interrogates the captured Caspian Marine Lieutenant Vincit 'Yeeks' Danyeko of the 17th Marines. Cimber presses Danyeko to explain why his reconnaissance flight entered the system, ignored hails, scanned the Imperial station, and attempted to flee, noting that the pilot's overheard talk of imminent death and a rigged suicide device make his story suspicious. Danyeko, drilled in disciplined evasion, insists with deadpan composure that he was merely on a cartographical survey assignment cataloging planets, moons, and asteroids, repeatedly needling the admiral that the Imperial fleet ruined his work. As Cimber circles toward the truth he is really after, that Danyeko came to spy on the battlestation, the lieutenant coyly claims the Death Star registered as just another moon on his sensors. The verbal duel ends at an impasse, with Cimber signaling his security men and implying that more forceful methods may follow.
